svg.aria-label: Memory as an action: a self-editing core memory block. On the left, the core block user_profile holds name Dana, prefers email. A memory_replace tool call, carrying the Part 1 typed-schema contract with block, old, and new arguments, passes through a validate() gate. The gate confirms the args are present and typed and that the block exists, then fires, replacing prefers email with prefers phone. On the right, the block now holds name Dana, prefers phone. Below, a second call targets an unknown block called preferences. It is schema-valid but the runtime rejects it, returning an error as an observation rather than crashing, so the block is left unchanged.
svg.title: Memory as an action: a self-editing core block, validated then fired, with an unknown block rejected

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: Memory as an action: the agent rewrites its own core block with a validated tool call

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: Every tool through Part 5 was read-only. memory_replace is a first-class tool with the Part 1 contract: typed schema, validated before it fires.

svg.text: An invalid edit is rejected AS an observation, not a crash (error-as-observation, Part 2)

svg.text: The shape is fine, but the named block does not exist. The runtime returns an error string; the agent reads it and the state is left untouched.

svg.text: memory_append and memory_replace turn memory into an ACTION the agent takes, not just state it reads. They carry the same Part 1 contract as

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: every other tool: a typed schema, validated before firing. A valid call rewrites a labeled core block in-loop; an invalid one returns an error

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: as an observation. This is MemGPT and Letta by hand: the controller editing its own persistent memory through the same tool loop.
